footer.tagline: Where children discover the musician inside.

legal.translationDisclaimer: This document has been translated into Spanish for convenience. In the event of any discrepancy between the English and Spanish versions, the English version shall prevail.

home.heroTitle:
Your child is
already a
musician.
home.heroBody: At Creative Kids, children make real music from day one alongside professional musicians—building not just musical skill, but lasting confidence, creativity, and the ability to collaborate and show up with others for life.
home.heroImageAlt: A tree with musical instruments growing from its branches
home.workshopsDesc: No waiting—your child performs alongside professional musicians on day one.
home.campDesc: Music, nature, and stillness woven into one immersive week—they come home with a freedom to make music they've never felt before.
home.musicSchoolDesc: The place where music becomes theirs.

about.title: About
about.jackAlt: Jack Barben, founder of Creative Kids Music Project
about.bio1: Jack Barben has been playing music his whole life and teaching it for nearly thirty years. He got his start early—directing bands and choirs as a teenager, then studying piano, voice, and trombone in college, including piano at the Peabody Conservatory. He's directed music in churches for over twenty years, maintained a private studio for twenty-five, and continued exploring along the way: creating choirs and ensembles, immersing himself in traditions from around the world.
about.bio3: At Creative Kids, children make real music from day one. They jam, improvise, compose, and perform—not after years of preparation, but as the way in. Professional musicians play alongside them, modeling what can't be taught with words. Groove comes before theory. Listening before reading. Feel before technique.
about.bio4: Your child is already a musician. We help them discover that.
about.bio5: From their first session, they're inside the music—playing in flow with others, making real choices, alive in it now. Something shifts—in what they can do, and in who they know themselves to be. And what they build here—confidence, presence, the ability to show up with others—reaches far beyond any instrument.
about.ourHomeDesc: Creative Kids Music Project is hosted at St. Luke's / San Lucas Episcopal Church in the heart of Vancouver—a community that shares our belief in the formative power of music, nature, and creativity.
about.invitation: The Creative Kids Music Project is an invitation—to hear more, to notice more, and to bring more of ourselves to the world.
about.churchName: St. Luke's / San Lucas Episcopal Church
about.churchAddress: 426 E Fourth Plain Blvd, Vancouver, WA 98663
about.churchPhotoAlt: St. Luke's / San Lucas Episcopal Church

workshops.title: Workshops
workshops.headline: Your child makes real music on day one—not after a year of lessons.
workshops.body1: We start with the foundation: the beat. Feel it, move with it, keep it going. Then we add another layer—a loop, a pattern, a riff. Your child plays alongside professional musicians who hold the groove when they stumble and keep everything moving together.
workshops.body2: This is how we teach: we begin from success, supported by masters, and skill follows naturally.
workshops.dinnerTime: 6:30–7:00 PM Dinner — Parents, please join us!
workshops.location: St. Luke's / San Lucas Episcopal Church
workshops.address: 426 E Fourth Plain Blvd, Vancouver, WA

summerCamp.title: Summer Camp
summerCamp.headline: A week where music, nature, and play weave together into a single immersive experience.
summerCamp.body1: Each day begins outside—sitting in stillness, walking barefoot, listening for the farthest sound, the quietest sound. When the body is awake and the mind is open, music flows.
summerCamp.body2: Then we go deeper. Kids hold the bass line while others carry the melody. They add rhythm, create texture, write their own songs, pick up new instruments. Professional musicians are there all week, playing alongside them, and each day a guest instructor arrives, weaving in movement, voice, rhythm, composition—another thread added to the week.
summerCamp.finaleBody1: Friday evening, parents gather to share dinner and hear what their kids have been creating. Sunday morning, the children help create and transform the musical tapestry of the 10am St. Luke's service.
summerCamp.finaleBody2: What they carry home is bigger than any performance: a freedom to make music they've never felt before.
summerCamp.location: St. Luke's / San Lucas Episcopal Church
summerCamp.address: 426 E Fourth Plain Blvd, Vancouver, WA

musicSchool.title: Music School
musicSchool.headline: What if we brought music back into our lives? What if every child could sing, dance, feel rhythm, create songs? What if kids could learn to be creative and expressive at the same time as disciplined and focused? What if music became part of our lives again as something that nourished community, nourished our souls, nourished our hearts, and held us together as it has for thousands of years?
musicSchool.body1: Not long ago in this country, everyone sang together. We danced together. We had social gatherings where music was present and everyone participated. We are losing that world. Creative Kids Music hopes to bring it back into our community and our culture.
musicSchool.body2: The vision is built on a simple model of how music used to root and nourish us. It models language acquisition: immersion first, understanding later. It draws on what we know about how the brain actually learns, on techniques of concentration that develop both discipline and musical expressiveness, and on sources as varied as Alexander Technique, the work of Iain McGilchrist, meditation and body awareness, and the vast array of methods and techniques musicians have been using for hundreds of years. At Creative Kids Music, these tools and models take shape in the real world and the results speak for themselves.
musicSchool.bullet1: Kids are immersed in music three days a week.
musicSchool.bullet2: Kids develop a musical foundation that includes theory, relative pitch, rhythmic mastery, composition and improvisation techniques, and a working understanding of different musical genres.
musicSchool.bullet3: We emphasize playing. Kids learn by doing and imitating. Access to professional musicians in a variety of settings gives them multiple models and different ways of experiencing and expressing music.
musicSchool.bullet4: Kids put what they learn into practice through frequent, low-stress performance opportunities.
musicSchool.bullet5: Kids learn how to listen to each other, to respond in real time, and to make themselves and the people around them sound good.
musicSchool.bullet6: Kids develop discipline, concentration, patience, and diligence. They learn how to learn. They build confidence on the back of real skills. They develop the ability to listen to themselves, to assess honestly what they're doing, and to use that feedback to grow.
musicSchool.closing1: Creative Kids Music is designed to complement the variety of musical offerings throughout the Vancouver and Portland area. If your child takes private lessons, this program will deepen and enrich that work. If they don't, this is a foundation that will serve them wherever music takes them. What we're building is a culture of music, of community that connects through making music together, not just listening to it.
musicSchool.closing2: Our goal is to launch our after-school program in the fall of 2026. We need a minimum of 8 families committed to make it happen, and we'd love for you to consider being a part of it.
musicSchool.closing3: We need people who believe in this vision to help support scholarships. We need parents who want to help spread the word. We need music teachers and professionals who are looking to enhance and expand what they offer their students.
musicSchool.closing4: Please consider being a part of this. We can't do it without you.
musicSchool.sidebarDesc: We need approximately 8 committed families to make this program work for the 2026–2027 school year. Before we finalize the details, we want to hear from you.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: What ages do you work with?
faq.a1: Creative Kids programs are designed for rising 3rd through 8th graders (ages 9–13). We welcome all skill levels—from kids who've never touched an instrument to those who've been playing for years.
faq.q2: What if my child has never played an instrument?
faq.a2: Perfect. That's often the best place to start. Children who arrive without habits or expectations tend to listen more openly and jump in more freely. They'll be making music with the group in their first session.
faq.q3: What should my child bring to a workshop?
faq.a3: Comfortable clothes and a water bottle. If they play an instrument and want to bring it, they're welcome to—but it's not required. We provide everything else.
faq.q4: What should my child bring to summer camp?
faq.a4: Comfortable clothes they can move in, closed-toe shoes for outdoor time, a water bottle, and sunscreen. If they play an instrument and want to bring it, they're welcome to—but it's not required. We provide everything else.
faq.q5: What are the drop-off and pick-up details?
faq.a5: Drop-off and pick-up happen at St. Luke's Episcopal Church. Please come in the main entrance. We ask that parents arrive promptly.
faq.q6: What's the ratio of kids to instructors?
faq.a6: One professional musician for every 4–6 children.
faq.q7: What's your refund and cancellation policy?
faq.a7: Full refund if you cancel more than two weeks before the program start date. Within two weeks, we offer a 50% refund or credit toward a future program.
faq.a8: Creative Kids is hosted at St. Luke's Episcopal Church, a community that shares our belief in the formative power of music, nature, and creativity. The program is not religious, and all families are welcome. The partnership gives children something special: a real audience who shows up and genuinely celebrates what they create.

liability.title: Liability Waiver and Release of Claims
liability.assumptionIntro: I, the undersigned parent or legal guardian, acknowledge that participation in programs offered by Creative Kids Music Project ("the Program") involves certain inherent risks. These activities may include, but are not limited to:
liability.riskEquipment: Use of musical instruments and equipment
liability.riskGroup: Group activities, games, and movement exercises
liability.riskInteraction: Interaction with other participants and staff
liability.riskTransport: Transportation to and from program activities (if applicable)
liability.assumptionClose: I understand that these activities carry risks of personal injury, illness, property damage, or other harm. I voluntarily assume all such risks on behalf of my child.
liability.waiverIntro: In consideration of my child being permitted to participate in the Program, I hereby:
liability.waiverRelease: Release and discharge Creative Kids Music Project, its host organizations (including St. Luke's/San Lucas Episcopal Church, Vancouver, Washington), and their respective directors, officers, employees, volunteers, instructors, and agents (collectively, "Released Parties") from any and all claims, demands, actions, or causes of action arising out of or related to any loss, damage, or injury that may be sustained by my child while participating in Program activities.
liability.waiverWaive: Waive any claims I or my child may have against the Released Parties for any injury, illness, death, or property damage arising from participation in the Program, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
liability.waiverNoSue: Agree not to sue the Released Parties for any claims arising from my child's participation in the Program.
liability.waiverIndemnify: Indemnify and hold harmless the Released Parties from any claims, damages, or expenses (including attorney fees) arising from my child's participation in the Program.
liability.medicalIntro: In the event of an emergency, I authorize Program staff to:
liability.medicalTransport: Transport my child to a medical facility if necessary
liability.medicalClose: I understand that I am responsible for any medical expenses incurred on behalf of my child.
liability.accuracyBody: I acknowledge that emergency contact, medical, and authorized pickup information is collected separately during registration. I agree to keep this information current and to notify the Program promptly of any changes.
liability.photoBody: I understand that photographs, video, and audio recordings may be taken during Program activities. Media consent is addressed separately in the Media Release form.
liability.rulesBody: I agree that my child will follow all Program rules and instructions from staff. I understand that failure to follow rules may result in dismissal from the Program without refund.
liability.durationBody: For single-event programs (workshops, performances, or similar), this waiver applies to the specific event indicated during registration. For ongoing programs (music school sessions, summer camps, or similar), this waiver remains in effect for one year from the date of acceptance or until the conclusion of the registered program, whichever is later.
liability.severabilityBody: If any provision of this waiver is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.
liability.governingLawBody: This agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Washington.
liability.acknowledgmentIntro: By checking the acceptance box during registration, I acknowledge that:
liability.ackRead: I have read and understand this Liability Waiver
liability.ackParent: I am the parent or legal guardian of the child being registered
liability.ackAuthority: I have the legal authority to sign this waiver on behalf of my child
liability.ackAgree: I voluntarily agree to be bound by its terms
liability.ackDuration: This waiver remains in effect for all Program activities during the registered session(s)

programTerms.title: Terms & Conditions
programTerms.intro: By registering for a Creative Kids Music Project program, you agree to the following terms and conditions.
programTerms.registrationBody: Registration is complete when you have submitted all required forms and payment. Space is confirmed upon receipt of payment. Waitlist placement does not guarantee enrollment.
programTerms.accuracyBody: You agree to provide accurate information during registration and to notify us promptly of any changes to contact, medical, or emergency information.
programTerms.rulesBody: Participants are expected to follow all program rules and instructions from staff. We reserve the right to dismiss any participant whose behavior disrupts the program or poses a safety concern to themselves or others. In such cases, refunds are not guaranteed.
programTerms.supervisionBody: For drop-off programs, children must be signed in and out by a parent, guardian, or authorized pickup person. We are not responsible for supervision before the designated start time or after the designated end time.
programTerms.paymentBody: Full payment is due at the time of registration unless a payment plan is offered and selected. We accept credit/debit cards and other payment methods as indicated during registration.
programTerms.latePaymentBody: If payment fails or is not received, your registration may be canceled and your spot offered to another family.
programTerms.additionalFeesBody: Some programs may have optional add-ons (extended care, materials, etc.) with additional fees as indicated during registration.
programTerms.paymentPolicyBody: You may register for any program and pay at any time before the program begins. Payment is due before your child's first day of participation.
programTerms.refundPolicyBody: All payments are final. Once payment is made, no refunds will be issued. If you need to cancel before paying, simply cancel your registration through your account or contact us—there is no penalty.
programTerms.cancellationByUsBody: If we cancel a program due to insufficient enrollment or other circumstances, you will receive a full refund. We are not responsible for other costs you may have incurred (travel, childcare, etc.).
programTerms.weatherClosuresBody: If we must cancel a session due to inclement weather, power outage, or other emergency, we will notify registered families as soon as possible via email and text.
programTerms.makeUpBody: For multi-session programs, we will attempt to schedule a make-up session when possible. If a make-up cannot be scheduled, a prorated credit may be issued at our discretion.
programTerms.singleEventsBody: For workshops or single-day events canceled due to weather, we will reschedule if possible. If rescheduling is not possible, a full refund or credit will be issued.
programTerms.yourDecisionBody: If you choose not to attend due to weather conditions but the program is running as scheduled, no refund will be issued.
programTerms.howWeDecideBody: We consider road conditions, forecasts, and facility availability. If Vancouver Public Schools closes due to weather, we will likely cancel as well, but this is not automatic—we will communicate our decision directly.
programTerms.authorizedPickupBody1: Children will only be released to parents, guardians, or individuals listed as authorized pickup persons during registration. We may request photo ID for verification, especially for individuals we have not met before.
programTerms.authorizedPickupBody2: If someone not on your authorized list needs to pick up your child, you must notify us in advance (email or phone) with the person's name and relationship to your child.
programTerms.signInOutBody: For drop-off programs, children must be signed in at arrival and signed out at pickup. Please do not drop off children before the designated start time, as supervision is not available until then.
programTerms.latePickupIntro: We understand that delays happen. Please contact us as soon as possible if you will be late.
programTerms.latePickup15: Up to 15 minutes late: No charge, but please notify us
programTerms.latePickup30plus: More than 30 minutes late: $15 plus $1 per additional minute
programTerms.latePickupNote: Repeated late pickups may result in loss of enrollment privileges.
programTerms.mediaPreferencesBody: You may change your media permissions at any time by contacting the program director. Changes apply to future use; we make reasonable efforts to remove previously shared materials upon request, though we cannot control third-party reposts or cached content.
programTerms.mediaFamiliesBody: We cannot control photography by other families attending sessions or events. We ask all families to be respectful of others' privacy preferences and to avoid posting photos of other children to social media without permission from their parents. If you have concerns about your child appearing in other families' personal photos, please speak with us so we can address this at sessions.
programTerms.mediaEventsBody: Occasionally we host open events, performances, or showcases where visitors or press may be present and photography may occur. We will notify families in advance when such events are scheduled. Families who have opted out of media permissions may choose not to attend or may request accommodations.
programTerms.programUpdatesBody: We will communicate program information via the email address provided during registration. Please add our email address to your contacts to ensure you receive important updates.
programTerms.emergencyCommBody: In case of emergency, weather closure, or last-minute schedule changes, we will contact you via phone, text, or email as appropriate.
programTerms.liabilityBody: Participation in Creative Kids Music Project programs involves inherent risks. A separate <liabilityLink>Liability Waiver</liabilityLink> addresses assumption of risk, release of claims, and related matters. By completing registration, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to the Liability Waiver.
programTerms.changesToTermsBody: We may update these Terms & Conditions from time to time. Material changes will be communicated to registered families. Continued participation after changes are communicated constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
programTerms.governingLawBody: These terms shall be governed by the laws of the State of Washington.
programTerms.acknowledgmentIntro: By completing registration, you acknowledge that:
programTerms.ackRead: You have read and understand these Terms & Conditions
programTerms.ackLiability: You have read and agreed to the <liabilityLink>Liability Waiver</liabilityLink>
programTerms.ackParent: You are the parent or legal guardian of the child being registered
programTerms.ackAbide: You agree to abide by all program policies and rules
programTerms.ackResponsibility: You accept responsibility for ensuring your child follows program expectations
programTerms.contactIntro: Questions about these terms may be directed to:

behavior.title: Camper Behavior Agreement
behavior.valuesBody: Creative Kids Music Camp is a community where young musicians come together to learn, create, and grow. We believe that music-making is a collaborative experience that requires respect, kindness, and a willingness to support one another. These behavior expectations help us maintain a safe, positive, and productive environment for all campers.
behavior.respectKindness: Treat all staff, instructors, and fellow campers with kindness and respect
behavior.respectLanguage: Use appropriate language at all times (no profanity, put-downs, or hurtful comments)
behavior.respectListen: Listen when others are speaking or performing
behavior.respectCelebrate: Celebrate the efforts and achievements of fellow campers
behavior.respectInclude: Accept and include others, regardless of ability level or background
behavior.respectHands: Keep hands and feet to themselves
behavior.equipCare: Handle all instruments and equipment with care
behavior.equipInstructions: Follow instructions for proper use of instruments
behavior.equipPermission: Ask permission before using equipment that is not assigned to them
behavior.equipReport: Report any damage or issues to staff immediately
behavior.equipClean: Clean up their workspace and materials at the end of each activity
behavior.programSchedule: Follow the daily schedule and be on time for activities
behavior.programInstructions: Listen to and follow instructions from staff and instructors
behavior.programParticipate: Participate actively in all camp activities
behavior.programGroup: Stay with their assigned group unless given permission to leave
behavior.programElectronics: Keep personal electronics (phones, tablets, gaming devices) put away during camp hours unless authorized by staff
behavior.safetyAreas: Stay within designated camp areas at all times
behavior.safetyBuddy: Use the buddy system when going to restrooms during activities
behavior.safetyReport: Report any safety concerns or incidents to staff immediately
behavior.safetyEmergency: Follow all emergency procedures as instructed
behavior.safetyProhibited: Not bring prohibited items (weapons, drugs, alcohol, tobacco, or anything that could be used to harm others)
behavior.communicationBody: We believe in open communication with families. If there are any concerns about your child's behavior or experience at camp, please contact us right away. Similarly, we will reach out to you if we have concerns that need to be addressed together.
behavior.acknowledgmentIntro: By accepting this agreement during registration, I acknowledge that:
behavior.ackRead: I have read and understand the Camper Behavior Agreement
behavior.ackReviewed: I have reviewed these expectations with my child
behavior.ackFollows: My child agrees to follow these behavior expectations
behavior.ackSupport: I agree to support camp staff in maintaining a positive environment
